item.title: i know , i know - it's expensive, excessive and unpractical - but god i love it!
item.description:
Savoy Castle, Italy
Palazzo Castiglioni in Milán
Livraria Lello, located in Porto, Portugal, founded in 1906 by brothers José and António Lello.
Interior with stained glass window in the Casa Jaume Forn , Barcelona by the architect Jeroni Granell in 1904.
STAINED GLASS DOOR, 1908, at Palau Mornau (Hash Marihuana & Hemp Museum), Barcelona, Spain Built in the 16th century and restored in 1908 . Architect: Manuel Raspall
Art Nouveau door of a niche in the Recoleta Cemetery , Buenos Aires, Argentina
THE ROYAL ARCADE ,Norwich -Norfolk- England- UK. Elegant commercial gallery in Art Nouveau style inaugurated in 1899. Architect:George Skipper
Alexandre Dumas' home in Marly le Roi, France, ca. 1846. He named it Château d'If after a setting from his novel Le Comte de Monte-Cristo.
NTERIOR LOBBY at Casa Santurce, 1905, Carrer de València 293, Barcelona, Spain Architect: Miquel Madorell i Rius
La Samaritaine, Paris.
Stained glass window facade at CASA LLEO i MORERA , Barcelona.
Art Nouveau seahorse staircase banister. Rotterdam.
Rue Nancy. Brussels.
Corner Fred/Drot…Stockholm

item.title: Two Radiant Australian Cuttlefish
item.description:
Apparently these two are having a "come at me bro" moment showing off their color range to each other.
The smaller one knows his role and is going to fuck off after he's done peacocking
I hope these two brighten up your day. Source: https://www.instagram.com/terencetong_/

item.title: Richard Feynman and Penn & Teller
item.description: Source: Penn Jillette, God, No! Signs You May Already be an Atheist and Other Magical Tales (New York: Simon and Schuster, 2011), pp. xv-xvi, 156-157. Penn & Teller photo By Greg Dorais - Own work; cropped from File:Penn and Teller with James Randi TAM-2012.JPG, CC BY-SA 3.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=22993030 https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/ Richard Feynman photo By The Nobel Foundation - http://www.nobelprize.org/nobel_prizes/physics/laureates/1965/feynman-bio.html, PD-Sweden. https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?curid=34664654 “Penn & Teller, Penn Jillette and Teller, are American magicians, entertainers, and scientific skeptics who have performed together since the late 1970s. They are noted for their ongoing act that combines elements of comedy with magic. […] “In one of their most politically charged tricks, they make an American flag seem to disappear by wrapping it in a copy of the United States Bill of Rights, and apparently setting the flag on fire, so that ‘the flag is gone but the Bill of Rights remains’. The routine may also feature the ‘Chinese bill of rights’, presented as a transparent piece of acetate. They normally end the trick by restoring the unscathed flag to its starting place on the flagpole; however, on a TV guest appearance on The West Wing, this final part was omitted. The methods of the trick were revealed by the duo in an episode of Fool Us.” https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penn_%26_Teller “Richard Phillips Feynman was an American theoretical physicist. He is known for the work he did in the path integral formulation of quantum mechanics, the theory of quantum electrodynamics, the physics of the superfluidity of supercooled liquid helium, and in particle physics, for which he proposed the parton model. For his contributions to the development of quantum electrodynamics, Feynman received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1965 jointly with Julian Schwinger and Shin'ichirō Tomonaga.” https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_Feynman Penn & Teller Official Website https://pennandteller.com Penn & Teller Official YouTube Channel https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCX9N-aGQckrkpfzepUjQcYw
item.dc:creator: oukid

item.title: If I can do it, you can too!
item.description: Been drinking pretty heavy for last 8 years " my dads death". At my worst I was drinking around 18-24 beers a day. I had been down to around 6-12 a day. But finally got fed up with it. I was gonna lose the most important thing in my life. My wife! I was on a path to self destruction. So I swallowed my pride and went to the er. Told them what was going on. My drinking my anxiety etc. I was able to quit cold turkey that day. I was on a few meds to help with the cravings and the withdraw symptoms for the first few weeks. I'm not gonna lie to ya. I still have cravings to drink. But they aren't very often. I just tell myself " Hey, we dont do that anymore". Then I refer to the list of reasons I don't drink. 1.i don't want my kids growing up calling their dad a drunk 2.i grew up in a house of drinkers. Listening to my parents fight. I don't want my family like that 3.money 4.takes my time away from my wife/kids/stuff around the house 5.not a healthy long term habit 6.not a good example for my kids 7.i drink for the wrong reasons 8. I turn into a different person 9.i drink to relieve stress, then it causes more stress 10.makes me focus on the negative thoughts more 11.hungover all the time 12.i don't need a crutch in life for bad times or to have fun 13. Not fair to my family or myself or friends 14. Alcoholism runs in my family 15. I have a hard time controlling my anger 16.hard to handle unexpected stress/problems 17. I pee alot / abusing my liver and kidneys 18. I don't want to be that person anymore 19. I'm a better person sober 20. I don't want my kids growing up having issues with alcholic 21. When my my wife looks into my eyes, I want her to know it's the real me looking back at her. 22. Dui would eventually happen / kill someone or myself. I just wanted to post this as hope for other people. If I can do it. So can you! You are stronger than you think! You are a better person sober. You don't need booz to deal with life. If you want to chat that's cool. I'm gonna keep putting one foot in front of the other and stay focused. Small steps small changes turn into big changes. Thank you for reading. Have a great day!
item.dc:creator: smashole

item.title: Gym tip.

item.dc:creator: countervail247365

item.title: I can't even draw straight tic tac toe lines
item.description: "Rajacenna, a Dutch artist celebrated for her hyper-realistic pencil drawings, captivates audiences with her extraordinary ability to create multiple artworks simultaneously using both hands and feet. Known as a "quadridextrous" artist, her unique skill blurs the boundary between art and photography, drawing attention worldwide for its intricate detail. Through a specialized technique, she reaches peak focus while appearing relaxed—an “Einstein brain signature” observed in her EEG scans. Artist (IG): rajacenna"
item.dc:creator: 13andido
